card.intuition: A keyword that refers to the 'context' in which a function is currently executing. Its value is not fixed and depends on HOW the function was called.
card.analogy: The word 'Me': if Alice says 'Look at me', 'me' refers to Alice. If Bob says 'Look at me', 'me' refers to Bob. The word 'me' changes based on who is speaking (the caller).
when_to_use: Accessing object properties from within its own methods
Using arrow functions to capture the 'lexical' this (like in class components or event handlers)
gotchas: Arrow functions do NOT have their own 'this'; they inherit it from the code that created them
Global binding: if you call a standalone function in a non-strict mode browser, 'this' defaults to the `window` object

exercise.prompt: Identify the result of 'this' when using .call().
hints: When you manually tell JS what 'this' should be, it is 'Ex______'
